subject: feat(observability): log auth-bypass, API errors, and realtime channel failures
body:
Tighten the observability surface so support + devs can tell from the
DebugPill buffer what actually happened during a session:

- src/config/authBypass.ts: emit a boot entry (debugLog 'boot' 'warn')
  in addition to the existing console.warn, so exported debug reports
  always carry the auth-bypass state unambiguously.
- src/api/client.ts: collapse apiGet/Post/Put/Delete into a shared
  `request()` helper that logs every network failure and every non-ok
  HTTP response to debugLog('graph', 'error', …). Removes the duplicated
  error-handling path in apiDelete.
- src/hooks/*.ts (8 files): every realtime channel subscribe callback
  now logs CHANNEL_ERROR and TIMED_OUT statuses. Previously these were
  silent — users saw stale data with no clue why.

Also add a GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/guard-auth-bypass.yml)
that fails pushes and PRs to main when AUTH_BYPASS_ENABLED is true.
Feature branches are not checked, so dev work with the shim can continue
unhindered. The gate is a safety net for when the flag is eventually
flipped back off — prevents accidental re-enablement.

Tags: observability, debug, error-handling, ci
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: cross-fleet-docs
Semver: patch
title: log auth-bypass, API errors, and realtime channel failures
